expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: final quality pass preserves layering and executable wording
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly centers the case focus: it states the benchmark evaluates “Phase 6 contract emphasis: a final quality pass that preserves canonical layering and executable wording” and lists the rubric’s canonical 6-layer model plus executable wording checks; it also notes the focus “cannot be demonstrated” due to missing artifacts, but the focus itself is explicitly covered.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase6
expectations.evidence: Both execution_notes.md and result.md are organized around Phase 6: they enumerate Phase 6 responsibilities (spawn manifest handling, running phase6.sh then --post, stopping on non-zero), required Phase 6 artifacts (phase6_spawn_manifest.json, drafts/qa_plan_phase6_r<round>.md, context/quality_delta_<feature-id>.md), and Phase 6 rubric requirements (layering audit, preservation statements). The conclusion is framed as “Blocked / Insufficient evidence to demonstrate Phase 6 compliance,” which is still Phase-6-aligned.
